# Daemon Local QA Candidate Decision

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/localqacandidatedecision -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

Executable SSOT: [`local-qa-candidate-decision.riido.json`](local-qa-candidate-decision.riido.json).

- evidence artifact: `local-qa-candidate-decision`
- decision records: `22`

## Assertions

- every local QA closed-loop candidate must have exactly one decision record
- decision records without a matching candidate are stale loop residue and must fail verification unless candidate_scope is local_observed
- decision records must name owner, priority, disposition, next loop, next artifact, and reason
- triage-required and deferred decisions must name review_by
- decision next_artifact must be one of the candidate required_next_artifacts

## Loop

- Observation: Local QA run evidence can expose closed-loop candidates while prioritization still lives in human memory.
- Hypothesis: A daemon-owned candidate decision sidecar can force every current candidate to bind owner, priority, disposition, next loop, and next artifact.
- Execute: Verify the generated decision reader, then consume out/local-qa-run.json from the same local-qa-runner workflow and publish decision evidence.
- Evaluate: The verifier fails on missing candidate input, missing candidate decisions, orphan decisions, incomplete decision metadata, or next_artifact values outside the candidate's required_next_artifacts.
- Retrospective: Harnesses still explore broadly, but their findings cannot remain passive reports after local QA produces a candidate list.
